outcome_contract.why: Reception's theme is the last major app still using a hand-authored token file. Unifying both apps on the compiler pipeline eliminates a category of drift — colour changes need one edit in `tokens.ts` and the generated file stays in sync.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Reception's theme tokens are compiled by `generateThemeCSS` and the generated CSS file matches the current hand-authored `tokens.css` in both content and runtime behaviour. Both parity tests pass in CI.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Shade hsl() format fix + Tailwind bridge change ensures bar/POS grid cells render correctly; semantic tokens unchanged
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Visual spot-check of shade colours in dev required before merge

engineering_coverage.applicable: Dark mode toggle (`html.theme-dark`) and media query preference both reproduced in generated file
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Both dark blocks tested by TASK-03

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — CSS-only change, no auth or data handling
N/A — CSS file replacement with no runtime side effects
Two new parity test files; CI gate before import swap
`ThemeCSSConfig` interface unchanged; `tokenVarMap` is an existing field; `-dark` suffix siblings preserved in `:root` for alias consumers
Generated file equivalent size to current `tokens.css` (~294 lines); no runtime cost change
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Additive only: `--theme-transition-duration` var is new but unused by reception consumers today

engineering_coverage.applicable: New generated file is additive until TASK-05 swaps the import; rollback = revert `globals.css` import line

stage_payload.summary: Reception is the last major app still using a hand-authored `tokens.css` generated by a separate script. This plan retrofits reception to use the shared `generateThemeCSS()` compiler pipeline via a purpose-written `theme-css-config.ts`. The compiler already supports `tokenVarMap` and `darkSelector`, which are the two key extension points this plan uses. The generated CSS file is a known superset of the current `tokens.css`: it adds two properties (`color-scheme: light/dark` and `--theme-transition-duration: 200ms`) that the compiler always emits but `tokens.css` does not contain. All token var
goals: Create `packages/themes/reception/src/theme-css-config.ts` mapping all tokens to the compiler via `tokenVarMap`.

Generate `apps/reception/src/styles/theme-tokens.generated.css` with all token vars from current `tokens.css` plus two known additive properties (`color-scheme` and `--theme-transition-duration`) that the compiler always emits.
Add `packages/themes/reception/__tests__/generated-parity.test.ts` and `coverage-parity.test.ts`, both passing in CI.
Wire the generated file into `apps/reception/src/app/globals.css` replacing the direct `tokens.css` import.
Update `tailwind.config.mjs` shade bridge to use `var(...)` instead of `hsl(var(...))` for shade entries, since shade vars now contain full `hsl()` values.
non_goals: Do not change any colour values — pipeline retrofit only, no rebrand.
Do not migrate `globals.css` `--reception-*` runtime aliases into the compiler.
Do not add or remove shade families.
Do not change the `ThemeCSSConfig` interface in `@themes/base`.
Do not touch the existing `tokens.css` file — it stays as-is until TASK-05 swaps the import.
constraints: Shade tokens must emit as full `hsl()` strings in the generated file (e.g., `hsl(330 55% 66%)`), not bare triplets — Tailwind v4 cascade rule.
Semantic tokens keep bare HSL triplets (consumed via `hsl(var(...))` in Tailwind config).
selected_approach: `theme-css-config.ts` with `tokenVarMap` populated from `tokens.ts` — both base vars and `-dark` suffix vars as flat entries; shade light/dark values wrapped in `hsl()`.
Generate script: call `generateThemeCSS()`, then post-process the output to emit a second `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) :root { ... }` block with identical dark var assignments.
Include `--theme-transition-duration: 200ms` in generated file (additive, harmless, expected from profile).
Why planning is not reopening option selection:
Compiler already has `tokenVarMap` and `darkSelector` — the prerequisite work is confirmed complete.
Option B (keep dark blocks manual in globals.css) would leave a partial migration; the generate-script post-processing approach achieves full migration more cleanly.

task_briefs.title: Swap `globals.css` import and update Tailwind shade bridge

areas.label: Token authoring
areas.flow: 1. Edit `tokens.ts` → 2. Edit `theme-css-config.ts` if structure changes → 3. Run `pnpm --filter scripts reception:generate-theme-tokens` → 4. Commit both `theme-css-config.ts` and `theme-tokens.generated.css` → 5. CI parity test validates committed file matches compiler output

areas.label: Dark mode
areas.trigger: User toggles theme or OS preference triggers dark mode
areas.flow: `html.theme-dark` class (explicit toggle) → generated file `html.theme-dark { ... }` block activates; system media query → `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) :root { ... }` block activates
areas.seams: Both blocks must be validated in dev

areas.label: CI validation gate
areas.flow: `generated-parity.test.ts` reads committed file + calls compiler; fails if any var mismatches → blocks merge
